input.event_description: The CT scan was processed by the radiology triage software (AI algorithm). The software did not flag the scan as positive for large vessel occlusion. Patient presented with acute stroke symptoms. The stroke was later identified by the radiologist on routine read.
input.mfr_narrative: Algorithm review indicates the LVO was at the periphery of the detection map.
input.extracted_failure_mode: False negative for large vessel occlusion detection
input.extracted_device_problem: Algorithm failed to flag peripheral LVO on CT scan
input.ai_related_rationale: Narrative explicitly references AI algorithm and detection map

expected_output.category_rationale: Narrative describes algorithm not flagging an LVO that was present and later identified on radiologist read — canonical false-negative against ground truth
evidence_quotes_contains_any: did not flag the scan as positive
stroke was later identified by the radiologist

notes: Textbook false_negative_clinical: AI missed, ground truth confirmed by clinician follow-up.

input.event_description: Radiology triage AI software flagged the scan as high-priority for pulmonary embolism. Physician relied on the alert and initiated anticoagulation. Subsequent CT angiography was negative for PE. Patient developed a minor hematoma at the access site.
input.mfr_narrative: Algorithm met its labeled sensitivity target; this was a false positive within expected operating characteristics. Physician procedure noted that PE flag was the primary driver of the treatment decision.
input.extracted_failure_mode: False positive for pulmonary embolism detection
input.extracted_device_problem: Algorithm produced false positive within labeled operating characteristics
input.ai_related_rationale: Narrative explicitly involves AI triage software

expected_output.category_rationale: AI flagged PE; subsequent CTA was negative — canonical false positive. Clinician deferral to AI alert is a secondary mode (automation_bias)
evidence_quotes_contains_any: flagged the scan as high-priority for pulmonary embolism

notes: Multi-mode case. Primary chosen by causal proximity (FP came first); automation_bias is the downstream amplifier.

input.event_description: Patient records were automatically and incorrectly merged with unrelated patient records. Two distinct patients' imaging studies and reports were linked under a single MRN. Discovered during routine quality audit; no immediate clinical action was taken on the wrong record.
input.mfr_narrative: Software anomaly in Brainlab Origin Data Management module led to automatic incorrect merges of unrelated patient records.
input.extracted_failure_mode: Automatic incorrect merge of unrelated patient records
input.extracted_device_problem: Software anomaly causing automatic incorrect merges
input.ai_related_rationale: Narrative describes software anomalies and automatic incorrect merges

expected_output.category_rationale: Wrong-patient record merge is the canonical data pipeline error; failure is in input data routing/integrity, not in clinical inference
evidence_quotes_contains_any: automatically and incorrectly merged with unrelated patient records

notes: Recurrent pattern observed in pilot — Brainlab ODM merge issue. Correctly placed in data_pipeline_error, not false_negative_clinical.

input.event_description: AI workstation power cord was damaged. Device would not power on. Replacement unit provided to facility.
input.mfr_narrative: Cord wear consistent with 18 months of normal use.
input.extracted_failure_mode: Power cord damage causing workstation power loss
input.ai_related_rationale: Power cord failure on the workstation; no algorithmic component implicated

expected_output.category_rationale: Failure is mechanical wear of a power cord on the workstation hosting the AI; no software or algorithmic component involved

notes: Tests honoring the upstream extractor. Even though product_code QIH is an AI device code, this Skill must not force the record into an AI taxonomy.

input.event_description: Quarterly performance audit identified that the mammography CAD software's sensitivity for invasive ductal carcinoma has declined from 88% at deployment to 76% over the past 14 months. Trend has been consistent across all reading sites. Subgroup analysis pending.
input.mfr_narrative: Investigation of training-data distributional drift initiated. Performance trend confirmed across multiple sites.
input.extracted_failure_mode: Sensitivity decline from 88% to 76% over 14 months
input.extracted_device_problem: Algorithm performance trend below labeled sensitivity
input.ai_related_rationale: Narrative explicitly describes performance decline of AI algorithm over time

expected_output.category_rationale: Narrative provides longitudinal sensitivity decline (88% → 76% over 14 months) across multiple sites — meets temporal-pattern requirement for drift
evidence_quotes_contains_any: declined from 88% at deployment to 76% over the past 14 months

notes: Drift class requires temporal evidence per §6 consistency rule. This narrative supplies it explicitly.
